steps.title: Distribute the negative sign
steps.content:
Apply the minus sign to each term in the second parentheses. When subtracting, we change the sign of each term:
$$(4x^3 - 5x^2 + 3) - (6x^3 + 2x^2 - x)$$
$$= 4x^3 - 5x^2 + 3 - 6x^3 - 2x^2 + x$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group by powers of $x$ and combine coefficients:

  • $x^3$ terms: $4x^3 - 6x^3 = -2x^3$
  • $x^2$ terms: $-5x^2 - 2x^2 = -7x^2$
  • $x$ term: $x$
  • Constant: $3$

$$-2x^3 - 7x^2 + x + 3$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
